user_message: Quick one -- draft the follow-up on Tailspin Toys. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 15:04 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Tailspin Toys QBR preparation

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-07 16:56 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 11:14 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-02-10
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Michelle (Tailspin Toys)

[00:01:30] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into QBR preparation.

[00:06:00] Michelle (Tailspin Toys): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:08:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Michelle (Tailspin Toys): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Michelle (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:24:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:26:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-G trap (Misattributes attendee role); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-G failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-G failure mode (Misattributes attendee role)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-G -- Misattributes attendee role
